text: Continuous Integration is a software development practice where developers frequently merge their code changes into a shared repository. Each time this happens, automated builds and tests are run. This is done through a CI pipeline, which is a set of steps that check the new code for errors. The main idea is to catch issues early, before they become bigger problems. By running tests automatically, the team can be confident that the new code works well with the existing codebase. This saves time and effort because problems are found and fixed quickly. Overall, CI helps make software development more efficient and reliable.
